dialogues.text: Though Andrés Bonifacio's life was tragically cut short, his unwavering commitment to independence and his role in igniting the Philippine Revolution left an indelible mark on history.

scenes.name: The Spark That Ignited a Nation

dialogues.text: Bonifacio, the "Father of the Philippine Revolution," awakened a nation's spirit. His cry for liberty, born in the streets of Tondo and amplified at Pugad Lawin, became the rallying call for a people determined to be free from colonial oppression.
The ideals he championed within the Katipunan – equality, justice, and mutual aid – continued to inspire the struggle for freedom, even as the path forward became more complex.

His courage, his vision, and his ultimate sacrifice laid the foundation for the Philippines' eventual independence. Andrés Bonifacio remains a symbol of the Filipino people's resilience and their unyielding desire for self-determination.
His legacy is etched not only in the annals of history but in the very heart of the nation he fought to liberate
